name: Mafdet
description: Mafdet is an ancient Egyptian goddess often depicted with the head of a cheetah, lynx, or cat. She is a protector goddess, particularly known for her role in protecting against venomous creatures and dangerous animals, such as snakes and scorpions. Mafdet's origins trace back to the Early Dynastic Period of Egypt, making her one of the oldest known deities in the Egyptian pantheon. Her powers include swift justice, protection from poisons and venom, and the ability to hunt down and punish wrongdoers. She was believed to possess the ability to run faster than the wind and to strike down her enemies with speed and precision. Mafdet was often invoked in spells and rituals aimed at warding off evil and ensuring safety. She played a significant role in the afterlife beliefs of the Egyptians, where she was believed to protect the deceased from harm and guide them through the underworld. While not as widely worshipped as some other Egyptian gods and goddesses, Mafdet's importance as a protector and dispenser of justice should not be overlooked. Her association with feline qualities reflects the Egyptians' reverence for animals and their understanding of the natural world. She served as a guardian of sacred places and royal chambers, ensuring the safety of the pharaoh and the stability of the kingdom.
subtitle: Goddess of Justice and Protection

keyMyths.title: Protection from Snakes
keyMyths.description: Mafdet is called upon to protect individuals from the venomous bites of snakes, utilizing her swiftness and power to neutralize the threat. She is a guardian against these dangers.

keyMyths.title: Punishment of Wrongdoers
keyMyths.description: Mafdet acts as an executioner of the gods, swiftly hunting down and punishing those who have committed crimes against the divine order. Justice is swift and unforgiving.

keyMyths.title: Royal Protector
keyMyths.description: Mafdet protects the pharaoh and the royal chambers from harm, ensuring the safety and security of the ruler and the stability of the kingdom. Her presence is a safeguard against treachery.
